meaning: adj.粗野的,不文明的,失礼的
examples: He was looking up into the sky all the time he was speaking, and this Alice thought decidedly uncivil.
This seemed uncivil, but perhaps wasn't.

Gary Tobin, author of The Uncivil University says it happens all the time.
Obama and Republican nominee Mitt Romney had been invited to a similar forum last fall, but Warren canceled it, saying the campaign had become too uncivil.
